Abstract
The Priority Project 'Transition of COSMO to ICON' (PP C2I) was launched in 2018.
The aim of the project is to ensure a smooth transition for the Consortium for Small Scale
Modelling (COSMO) national meteorological services (NMS) to the ICON model. This report
provides the rst published verication results of the COSMO NMS in the scope of C2I and
marks a starting point for further analysis and joint conclusions.
The verication results show improved surface scores for ICON. For precipitation scores in
the convection-permitting congurations, the results are neutral to positive.
Keywords: C2I, Verication
Correspondence to: D. Rieger (daniel.rieger@dwd.de)
Description Priority Project C2I
The Priority Project 'Transition of COSMO to ICON' (PP C2I) was launched in 2018. At that
time, the Consortium for Small Scale Modelling (COSMO) science plan called for a harmonization
of development of the COSMO and ICON (ICOsahedral Nonhydrostatic) models in the time
horizon of 2020. Also at that time, DWD reduced further development of the COSMO model to
the level needed for operational production. It was therefore necessary for the COSMO consortium
to start the migration to ICON-LAM (ICON - Limited Area Model) as the future operational
model.
In the meantime, the limited area, high-resolution model ICON-D2 was introduced for opera-
tional numerical weather forecasts on 10 February 2021. With this, an era of more than 20 years
of COSMO-model forecasts ended in Germany. The preoperational phase of ICON-D2 at DWD
showed, that ICON-D2 outperforms the previous operational setup COSMO-D2 in terms of the
quality of the results as well as in terms of computational eciency.
It has to be pointed out that ICON-D2 already contains a major bugx compared to the ICON
versions used in this study. A factor cp/cv was missing in the calculation of the temperature
4 Reports on ICON - Issue 006
C2I Verification Report D. Rieger et al.
tendency from the turbulence parameterization. This in turn led to major changes in the congu-
ration. The most prominent change is probably the usage of the radiation scheme ecRad (Hogan
and Bozzo, 2018, Rieger et al., 2019) instead of the previously used RRTMG (Mlawer et al., 1997).
An update to a version containing this bugx (  icon-2.6.2.2) is planned for the COSMO services
and improvements in the verication scores are expected which are not part of this report.
This project C2I represents a major step in the implementation of the COSMO strategy re-
garding the evaluation of ICON-LAM and the transition to ICON. The overall goal is to ensure a
smooth transition from the COSMO model to ICON-LAM within a four-year period. At the end
of the PP C2I, each participating institution shall have reached a state, where ICON-LAM may
replace the COSMO model in their operational forecasting system.
The participants of PP C2I are the national meteorological services of Greece, Israel, Italy,
Poland, Romania, Russia and their local partners. With the national meteorological service
of Brasil, INMET, one of the COSMO licensees also joined the project. In addition, a close
collaboration with the CLM Community has been established.
Figure 1: Illustration of the Priority Project C2I time line.
The time line of the project C2I is depicted in gure 1. An ICON training course and a workshop

catalog_entry.summary: This report evaluates the ICON model in limited area mode at various national meteorological services, providing verification results for Greece, Israel, Italy, Poland, Romania, and Russia.
